name: copilot
description: 👩‍✈️ Launch and manage containerized applications on AWS

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Create a new ECS or App Runner application

options.description: Deploy your service or job to a "test" environment
The location of an existing Docker image
Name of the service or job
The port on which your service listens
Optional. The number of times to try restarting the job on a failure

The schedule on which to run this job.  Accepts cron expressions of the format (M H DoM M DoW) and schedule definition strings.  For example: "0 * * * *", "@daily", "@weekly", "@every 1h30m". AWS Sche
Optional. The total execution time for the task, including retries. Accepts valid Go duration strings. For example: "2h", "1h30m", "900s"
Type of job or svc to create

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: Open the copilot docs

subcommands.name: app
subcommands.description: Commands for applications. Applications are a collection of services and environments

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a new empty application

options.description: Optional. Your existing custom domain name
Optional. Labels with a key and value separated by commas. Allows you to categorize resources

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: Lists all the applications in your account

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows info about an application

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete all resources associated with the application

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades the template of an application to the latest version

subcommands.name: env
subcommands.description: Commands for environments. Environments are deployment stages shared between services

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a new environment in your application

options.description: Optional. An AWS secret access key
Optional. An AWS session token for temporary credentials
Optional. Skip prompting and use default environment configuration
Optional. Use existing private subnet IDs
Optional. Use existing public subnet IDs
Optional. Use an existing VPC ID
Optional. Availability Zone names. (default 2 random AZs)
Optional. CIDR to use for private subnets. (default 10.0.2.0/24,10.0.3.0/24)
Optional. CIDR to use for public subnets.  (default 10.0.0.0/24,10.0.1.0/24)
Optional. Global CIDR to use for VPC. (default 10.0.0.0/16)
If the environment contains production services
Optional. An AWS region where the environment will be created

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: Lists all the environments in an application

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes an environment from your application

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows info about a deployed environment

options.description: Optional. Show the resources in your environment

subcommands.name: svc
subcommands.description: Commands for services. Services are long-running ECS or App Runner services

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a new service in an application

options.description: The location of an existing Docker image
Optional. Turn off selection for adding subscriptions for worker services
The port on which your service listens
Optional. SNS Topics to subscribe to from other services in your application. Must be of format '<svcName>:<topicName>'

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: Lists all the services in an application

options.description: Only show services in the workspace

subcommands.name: package
subcommands.description: Prints the AWS CloudFormation template of a service

options.description: Optional. Writes the stack template and template configuration to a directory

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys a service to an environment

options.description: Optional. Force a new service deployment using the existing image
Optional. Labels with a key and value separated by commas. Allows you to categorize resources

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a service from an application

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows info about a deployed service per environment

options.description: Optional. Show the resources in your service

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Shows status of a deployed service

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Displays logs of a deployed service

options.description: Optional. Only return logs before a specific date (RFC3339). Defaults to all logs
Optional. Specifies if the logs should be streamed
Optional. The maximum number of log events returned. Default is 10 unless any time filtering flags are set
Optional. Only return logs from specific log group
Optional. Only return logs newer than a relative duration like 5s, 2m, or 3h. Defaults to all logs
Optional. Only return logs after a specific date (RFC3339). Defaults to all logs
Optional. Only return logs from specific task IDs

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a running container part of a service

options.description: Optional. The command that is passed to a running container
Optional. The specific container you want to exec in. By default the first essential container will be used
Name of the service, job, or task group
Optional. ID of the task you want to exec in
Optional. Whether to update the Session Manager Plugin

subcommands.name: pause
subcommands.description: Pause running App Runner service

subcommands.name: resume
subcommands.description: Resumes a paused service

subcommands.name: job
subcommands.description: Commands for jobs. Jobs are tasks that are triggered by events

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a new scheduled job in an application

options.description: The location of an existing Docker image
Optional. The number of times to try restarting the job on a failure

The schedule on which to run this job.  Accepts cron expressions of the format (M H DoM M DoW) and schedule definition strings.  For example: "0 * * * *", "@daily", "@weekly", "@every 1h30m". AWS Sche
Optional. The total execution time for the task, including retries. Accepts valid Go duration strings. For example: "2h", "1h30m", "900s"

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: Lists all the jobs in an application

options.description: Only show jobs in the workspace

subcommands.name: package
subcommands.description: Prints the AWS CloudFormation template of a job

options.description: Optional. Writes the stack template and template configuration to a directory

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys a job to an environment

options.description: Optional. Labels with a key and value separated by commas. Allows you to categorize resources

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a job from an application

subcommands.name: task
subcommands.description: Commands for tasks. One-off Amazon ECS tasks that terminate once their work is done

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a one-off task on Amazon ECS

options.description: Path to the Docker build context
Optional. The short name or full ARN of the cluster to run the task in
Optional. The command that is passed to "docker run" to override the default command
Optional. The number of tasks to set up
Optional. The number of CPU units to reserve for each task
Optional. Run tasks in default cluster and default subnets
Optional. The entrypoint that is passed to "docker run" to override the default entrypoint
Optional. Environment variables specified by key=value separated by commas
Optional. The ARN of the role that grants the container agent permission to make AWS API calls
Optional. Specifies if the logs should be streamed
Optional. Generate a command with a pre-filled value for each flag. To use it for an ECS service, specify --generate-cmd <cluster name>/<service name>. Alternatively, if the service or job is created
The location of an existing Docker image
Optional. The amount of memory to reserve in MiB for each task
Optional. Architecture of the task. Must be specified along with 'platform-os'
Optional. Operating system of the task. Must be specified along with 'platform-arch'
Optional. Labels with a key and value separated by commas. Allows you to categorize resources
Optional. Secrets to inject into the container. Specified by key=value separated by commas
Optional. The security group IDs for the task to use. Can be specified multiple times
Optional. The subnet IDs for the task to use. Can be specified multiple times
Optional. The container image tag in addition to "latest"
Optional. The group name of the task.  Tasks with the same group name share the same set of resources.  (default directory name)
Optional. The ARN of the role for the task to use

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a running container part of a task

options.description: Optional. The command that is passed to a running container
Optional. Execute commands in running tasks in default cluster and default subnets
Name of the service, job, or task group
Optional. ID of the task you want to exec in
Optional. Whether to update the Session Manager Plugin

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a one-off task from an application or default cluster

options.description: Optional. Delete a task which was launched in the default cluster and subnets

subcommands.name: storage
subcommands.description: Commands for working with storage and databases

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a new AWS CloudFormation template for a storage resource

options.description: The database engine used in the cluster. Must be either "MySQL" or "PostgreSQL"
The initial database to create in the cluster
Optional. Attribute to use as an alternate sort key. May be specified up to 5 times. Must be of the format '<keyName>:<dataType>'
Name of the storage resource to create
Optional. Don't ask about configuring alternate sort keys
Optional. The name of the parameter group to associate with the cluster
Partition key for the DDB table. Must be of the format '<keyName>:<dataType>'
Optional. Sort key for the DDB table. Must be of the format '<keyName>:<dataType>'
Type of storage to add. Must be one of: "DynamoDB", "S3", "Aurora"
Name of the service or job to associate with storage

subcommands.name: secret
subcommands.description: Commands for secrets. Secrets are sensitive information that you need in your application

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Create or update secrets in SSM Parameter Store

options.description: Optional. A YAML file in which the secret values are specified
Optional. Whether to overwrite an existing secret
Values of the secret in each environment. Specified as <environment>=<value> separated by commas

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the version number

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Output shell completion code

subcommands.name: pipeline
subcommands.description: Commands for pipelines. Continuous delivery pipelines to release services

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates a pipeline for the services in your workspace

options.description: Environments to add to the pipeline
Branch used to trigger your pipeline
GitHub personal access token for your repository
(Deprecated.) Use --url instead. Repository URL to trigger your pipeline
The repository URL to trigger your pipeline. Supported providers are: GitHub, CodeCommit, Bitbucket

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Deploys a pipeline for the services in your workspace

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes the pipeline associated with your workspace

options.description: Deletes AWS Secrets Manager secret associated with a pipeline source repository

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows info about a deployed pipeline for an application

options.description: Optional. Show the resources in your pipeline

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Shows the status of a pipeline

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: Lists all the deployed pipelines in an application

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy a Copilot job or service

options.description: Optional. Force a new service deployment using the existing image
Name of the service or job
Optional. Labels with a key and value separated by commas. Allows you to categorize resources

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Help about any command
